payload.message:
Generate Keiran's daily morning investment brief for Telegram.

Focus areas (priority order):
1) Weekend weather: Sydney forecast for upcoming Saturday + Sunday (very brief).
2) Top AI/Tech News: Top 3 headlines in last 24h.
3) Portfolio watchlist: NVDA, MSFT, GOOGL, TSM, TSLA, AAPL, and MU (Micron).
4) Crypto: ETH/SOL/BNB quick move summary.
5) Macro + FX: STRICTLY keep this to 2-3 sentences total (not paragraphs). Only include the most decision-relevant updates for USD/AUD-exposed investors.

Important portfolio context:
- Portfolio update (latest): MSFT and TSLA positions are now exited (treat as zero-weight / watch-only unless Keiran says otherwise).
- MU and NVDA are now by far the largest portfolio slices and should receive the strongest focus in analysis.
- Keep GOOGL, AAPL, and TSM as secondary blue-chip watchlist items.
- Crypto is still smaller than blue-chip exposure overall.
- Emphasize what changed since yesterday and what matters most for MU + NVDA first.

Formatting rules (strict):
- Telegram-friendly, concise, high-signal.
- Main section headings must be:
  1) on their own line
  2) bold
  3) NOT bullets
  4) preceded by one blank line
  5) include one leading emoji for visual scan
- Under each heading, use bullet points only for content lines.
- Keep total length compact.

Use this exact structure:
Morning brief — <DAY DATE, TIME Sydney>

🌦️ **Weekend weather (Sydney):**
• ...
• ...

🤖 **Top AI/Tech News:**
• ...
• ...
• ...

📈 **Blue-Chip / FIAT Stocks:**
• ...
• ...
• ...
• ...
• ...
• ...
• ...

₿ **Crypto (24h):**
• ...
• ...
• ...
• Overall: ...

🌍 **Macro + FX:**
• ...
• ...
• ...

Use web search for current data.

payload.message:
Generate Keiran’s daily Cronulla dawn-run brief for right now.

Tone/personality:
- Energetic, spicy, playful, motivating.
- Keep it sharp and concise.
- Telegram-friendly.

Output format (strict):
- Start with one short greeting line: "Morning Keiran! Here's your Cronulla beach dawn-run intel:"
- Then output bullet lines in exactly this order and label style, with one emoji per line:
  • 🧭 HEADLINE:
  • 🌤️ WEATHER:
  • 💧 HUMIDITY:
  • 🌅 SUNRISE:
  • 🌧️ RAIN RISK:
  • 🌡️ TEMP (NEXT 2H):
  • 💨 WIND (NEXT 2H):
  • 🌊 TIDE:
  • 📌 CONFIDENCE:

Rating system for HEADLINE:
- ✅✅✅ = amazing run conditions
- ✅✅ = decent / good enough
- ✅ = marginal / caution
- ❌ = poor / likely skip

Scoring priorities (most to least important):
1) Rain risk in next 2 hours (deal-killer)
2) Tide status (lower is better; approaching low tide = positive)
3) Wind in next 2h (sustained + gusts; above 20 km/h unpleasant)

Deterministic decision rules (must follow):
1) If next-2h rain chance >= 40% OR explicit showers likely in next 2h => HEADLINE must be ❌.
2) Else if rain chance 15-39% => maximum HEADLINE is ✅ (never ✅✅ or ✅✅✅).
3) Else (rain chance < 15%):
   - If tide is dropping to low within next 3h AND sustained wind <= 18 km/h AND gust <= 28 km/h => ✅✅✅.
   - Else if sustained wind <= 22 km/h AND gust <= 32 km/h => ✅✅.
   - Else => ✅.
4) Never output ❌ when rain chance < 15% unless severe wind > 35 km/h gusts.

Consistency guardrail:
- If values are close to threshold (within ±3% rain or ±2 km/h wind/gust), reduce confidence and avoid overconfident wording.

Field requirements:
- WEATHER: concise condition summary only.
- HUMIDITY: current humidity percentage right now at the report instant.
- SUNRISE: local sunrise time for Cronulla today.
- RAIN RISK: explicit next-2h risk statement INCLUDING numeric probability % range.
- TEMP (NEXT 2H): temperature range expected over the next 2 hours.
- WIND (NEXT 2H): direction + sustained wind range over next 2 hours + gust speed.
- TIDE: no percentages. Use simple directional phrasing with next low/high and last opposite tide time.
- CONFIDENCE: one of High / Medium / Low with 3-6 words explaining uncertainty driver.

WIND line format is mandatory:
- "• 💨 WIND (NEXT 2H): <DIR> <SUSTAINED_MIN>-<SUSTAINED_MAX> km/h, gusting to <GUST> km/h"
- Never output N/A for gusts. If missing, estimate conservatively and output numeric km/h.

TIDE line format is mandatory:
- "• 🌊 TIDE: currently dropping to low at <TIME> (last high tide was at <TIME>)"
OR
- "• 🌊 TIDE: currently rising to high at <TIME> (last low tide was at <TIME>)"

Do not include tide percentages.
Do not add extra sections or paragraphs. Use current local weather + marine/tide data for Cronulla where available.
